item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from Shutterstock, which requires purchase for official use. Thus, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's still a novote from me, but I kinda preferred the old version of the conductor's pleas. It conveyed more desperation and I liked the hint about what phase two of the ritual was supposed to be.

That said, I headcanon that Zmey is descended from Xia Dynasty culture and ritual practices.

item.title: Article Update
item.content:encoded: I have updated this article to clean up some problematic issues and sections that weren't quite meeting my own standards.

item.title: Re: No musical in-jokes?
item.content:encoded:
• I don't understand what you're trying to say.
  • I don't think the exact number of times it's changed locus is extra relevant.
  • No, people have to hear the exact music being produced by the object (it's the only copy of that particular recording, anyway), and it has to be loud enough to hear the Impetus Point.
  • …What?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Contingency Apollo-B involves putting it in a vacuum, and it's only a last resort, because once the object is vacuum-contained, there will be no one to hear it, and it will be continuously getting stronger. So, in the event that the vacuum containment somehow fails, the energy produced by the object at that point would likely cause an instantaneous XK. So yeah, vacuum containment is only a last resort, because at that point it's all or nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unless it gets destroyed in space and transfers back to something on Earth. Contingency Apollo-B has to do with the vacuum, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As a translator I think the long long long sentence just beat the shxt out of me.
But it is really good.
I just want to cry.TAT
Yeah=_=.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Bringing it to the vacuum of space will do the trick.

item.title: No musical in-jokes?
item.content:encoded:
I love Sviridov. I love Winter Road. I respect (but do not love) 1812.

Therefore:

  • The description says the recording always produces Winter Road. The acquisition report says the original record was destroyed and the phenomenon hopped to a record of 1812. Presumably, that is the page image. However, common practice for possession-changing SCPs seems to be listing something to the effect of "currently housed in…" or "currently possessing the body of…", etc., in the containment procedures.
  • How many times did it change what it possesses? It's implied that it was a lot, so the above is extra relevant.
  • Is the containment broadcast required to be live? How will the SCP react to people listening to a different recording of Winter Road? If a digital broadcast is acceptable, can't its volume be lowered? Or that negates the containment effect?
  • The above would be related to a quiet easter egg about how Metal Gear Solid would be a containment measure for the SCP (as there are several SCPs contained by dissemination of knowledge of them), which would be a better joke than "be thankful it still played Winter Road after possessing a recording of 1812!".

I am yet too new to the site to vote, so now that the questions are out of my system, I will now go hide.

EDIT: I have returned, here's my downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Uh…. Why aren't they just keeping it suspended in a vacuum?

If there's no air to propagate the sound, there wouldn't be any sound… so..

I mean, you don't have to launch it into space, but building a vacuum containment unit should be a cinch for the foundation.

Or I'd swear there was a little space room SCP that was a vacuum with a little solar system. just have it orbit that teeny star.

I like it, don't get me wrong, but the containment procedures seem a little contrived for something that could be so easily contained.

And no, before it's suggested, I will not suspend enough of my belief to believe that it uses special scp magic to somehow emit sound through a vacuum.

Just.. no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Backstory seemed nice to me. Like everything here, raises more questions than answers; I'm hoping it's all there in the novelization.

item.title: Re: Edit Request
item.content:encoded:
Edit reverted.

The containment room is designed to withstand the potential force of the object (within reason; if it gets too loud, Apollo-B will be initiated), not the current force.

I appreciate your work, but I'll take care of the specific numbers.

Thanks for correcting the science.

item.title: Re: Edit Request
item.content:encoded:
ArthurWilborn, did you get permission to make the edit? If so, please make note of the staff member who authorized it.

In this case, you're fine since you're fixing a scientific inaccuracy, but in the future, if staff don't reply to your requests in a timely manner, please send a PM or make a forum thread instead of just going ahead with the fix.

(of course, if you did get permission and you just haven't noted it yet, then you can disregard this comment)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aside from the decibels problem already mentioned, I quite like this.

however, I did like it, and grasp it, as soon as I realised what it was, what it did, and why it was scary. I felt that all its backstory was just filler that left me a bit irritated. I don't feel it adds at all, and as for the twist, assuming I've figured it out right, it turned something from "what's going on?" and "Jeez, I'm glad we've got this one contained" to "oh, it's this again".

item.title: Edit Request
item.content:encoded: I'd like to fix the decibel figures to something that is closer to reality, since that bit isn't supposed to be the anomalous part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Related problem - it's current volume, "34.35dB", would barely be audible even if you were in the same room. That's as quiet as a whisper - normal speech is ~70dB. I would have to be around 160 dB (as loud as the space shuttle) to require the level of containment it has now.

Not to mention the issue of propagation. Decibels are logarithmic. A sustained sound of 180 decibels can be heard for hundred of miles. A sustained sound of 300 decibels could probably be heard anywhere on Earth. 700 would rival the power of a star.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmm, mind cluing me in a bit more about Apollo-B? I've tried, but I cant seem to figure it out. I've determined that it can't be a destruction procedure, as that would be fruitless, and it cant be broadcasting to all the Foundation sites, because that is
Apollo-A. The closest I can get is "Broadcast into a nearby town until problem can be resolved", but If you cant broadcast to the foundation sites, how would you broadcast there?
Edit: Nevermind, saw your previous post. My mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
IIRC real dB does not actually exceed 194 decibels. Anything beyond that is converted to other types of energy.

Still, I could accept 'SCP magic shenanigans' as an answer to bypass that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nuoh my gawd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh my god

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just realized that this skip is an XKCD.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Or toss it into another dimension. Like through 093. There are most definitely audio recording devices lying around there, so it stays there, even if the current carrier is destroyed.

Or transfer it to a device small enough and throw it through the "glory hole". It's their problem now.

Or just give it to one of the D-Class used to dial 120.

The possibilities to get rid of it are limitless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The lizard? Nope. It's intelligent and adaptive - from test logs we see it can even survive changed physical constants. For all that we know, when launched into space it could manifest small propulsion devices and just fly back to Earth. This is why using a nuke on it was prohibited by O5 council. This one is purely reactionary. The situation here is different.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Half of our Keter threats (including the lizard) could be solved by launching it into space.

Contingency Apollo-B is certainly related to vacuums.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shoot it into space.

Seriously, shoot it into space ("in space, noone can hear you scream"). Who cares if it gets louder and louder? Near complete vacuum that exists in the Solar system can't transmit even the loudest of sounds. If a stable orbit is deemed too dangerous (can be knocked down, etc.), just make an another Voyager and aim it at Alpha Centauri. And as for transfer upon destruction, make 20 copies of sound emitting device, send them along in probes (the distance to Earth will be greater). Put some noise detecting devices on the probes to determine the situation. Problem solved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And, of course, each person deafened by it is one person no longer listening to it, so it has to get louder…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It is unclear what mechanism produces sound from this object.

The disc isn't being played, the sound just sorta radiates from the object.

This is dedicated to Roget's 1591 and Reject's 1969; I have a thing for "XK devices", and wanted to see if I could make one using music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a couple of skips with a similar premise (1591 with shades of 332 before it was rewritten) but this works pretty well and Zmey are an intriguing organisation. I presume you can't simply not play current instances of 1748, otherwise you could just lock it in a box and call it a day; I don't think you actually identify what 1748 is currently coming from.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Змей". The last letter is different, and it should be probably capitalized, since it's a name of an organization.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I understand your point, but I doubt the Foundation would authorize more experiments because of how volatile this thing is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has an infinite capacity to get louder, and, once it reaches a certain point, it will keep getting louder regardless of how many people can hear it. Those sound waves will eventually cause earthquakes, tsunamis, etc, and eventually become loud enough to wipe out humanity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a very interesting SCP. However, maybe you could expand/clarify in the article why it's labeled Keter? I know people keep bugging you about that, but it was just one thing that bothered me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fixed the Russian translation (hopefully).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is it "змеы"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That does sound like snake/serpent/whatever so AFAIK sounds about right. That said, why not use the original cyrillic name for them in the article? They are a russian group after all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I might be mistaken about "Iamya"; would "Zmey" work?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, I made some alterations to the procedures and description; it should be more clear why this is XK/Keter.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But … russian folk tales, at least the ones I have read do have dragons, just as well as slovak ones do.
I thought the name was something not meaningful, or an acronym aka thirding what anqxyr and Gene say.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I second that. As a Russian, I'd really like to know what it is supposed to mean.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't upvote this for the reasons I gave in the draft stage - the complex backstory gives too much away and would be much more effective if you'd presented it some other way (say, an interview with the remaining Iamya witness), rather than just telling us how it all went down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it needs to be clearer about why it's dangerous, as I couldn't work it out untill I read the explaination on this talk page. It's a good idea, but it could do with being a little clearer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And, in all likelihood, instantly going deaf.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvote for the whole "Here is the SCP, but these addendums now suggest that something more is coming, which we're not really investigating even though it sounds really serious."
Been done too much in my opinion, it's actually a decent enough skip without having to add all that shite.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can you link me to anything about it, or the original Cyrillic spelling of the word? Because that's the fist time I've heard of it, and I keep thinking that maybe you mean Lamia, but that's spelled with "l" and isn't really Russian.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually considered this chain of thought during composition. If and when this were to occur, it would at the very least blow the Foundation's cover. How would you explain a vinyl disc producing sounds of this magnitude? In any case, someone would probably try to destroy it…in which case it relocates to the nearest device and causes even more destruction.

If a city suddenly hears an immensely loud recording of "Winter Road", they're not just gonna sit there and listen to it and think "oh, that's cool". It would cause chaos.

Also, the amount of people required gets higher as it gets louder…perhaps I should make that rate exponential and include a mathematical model (so even a city wouldn't be able to stem the growth).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The word "Iamya" doesn't translate directly as "dragon", it's the Russian mythological equivalent of a dragon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't like the effect and I don't care for the backstory.

I'm puzzled a bit by the dimensions of the containment chamber. Why is it so tall, and why is the distance between the primary and secondary containment chambers differs for different faces?

I don't know any Russian word that can be translated as "Dragon" and can be transcribed as Iamya. Actually, I don't know any Russian word that can be transcribed as Iamya.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It would grow loud enough to destroy every audio device capable of replicating the noise before cracking the Earth. It would (as Harland said) reach a major population center long before that.

And in the meantime it's perfectly well contained by D's being forced to listen to it like elevator music. Hell, you don't even have to tie them to chairs if you just increase the amount of people available for the rotation.

Sorry, I'm still unconvinced. Which would be less of a deal for me if this justification could be found more easily in the text.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Question.

If it is getting ever louder, presumably that means it can be heard from further away, which presumably means more people will be hearing it, making them part of the audience. Why would that not stem the growing noise? Once the sound gets loud enough, the sound may reach a major population center, and then boom, millions of people listening to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The danger is more so from the sound waves eventually becoming strong enough to generate earthquakes and tsunamis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It has an infinite and irreversible capacity to get louder unless it's supplied with a sufficient audience (which has to be bigger the louder it gets). It could, eventually, literally become loud enough to destroy the Earth (or at least wipe out civilization).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was my main problem with the piece, and I'm insufficiently convinced by the actual content of the article for it to change my opinion on the matter here.

I don't hate it, mind you, but as it is I don't like it. It doesn't strike me as a Keter object. If you can include some justification or make a change that solidifies this as a Keter object in my mind (or if you change the classification accordingly, perhaps) it might be enough to swing my vote.

Also, I really wish you had managed to do this without the involvement of a GoI.

Edit: no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The elaborate and detailed backstory of its creation actually kind of kills it for me. Also, the effect of "deafen til you die" reminds me a lot of SCP-808.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not bad, but the writing feels muddled somehow. The elaborate background story lends a nice touch, but it also almost pushes it into tale territory for me. As a fan of devious Russians, Iamya does speak to me, but it doesn't save it in the end.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ehhh. It's not really hitting a—no I am not going to.

It's not really catching my interest.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It keeps getting louder unless it has enough people listening to it (a number which increases as it gets louder). If left unchecked, it will eventually produce sound waves strong enough to cause geological damage (not to mention it would deafen people exposed to it, and deaf people are incapable of listening to it to stop the growth).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad. That said, I liked it better with AWCY in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Overall, it's just not jivin' with me.

Also, 1) how is this Keter? and 2) how would this cause an XK-Class scenario?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Much better sans AWCY. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one pretty good. One issue I have is that at the beginning the "Impetus Zone" thing kinda threw me off, and I had to reread that bit a couple times before I understood what was going on.
